questions.text: What is the national tree of India?
questions.explanation: The banyan tree is the national tree of India.

questions.text: How many colours are there in the Indian flag?
questions.explanation: The Indian flag has three colours — saffron, white, green.

questions.text: The national bird of India is the ____.
questions.explanation: The peacock is India's national bird.

questions.text: The lotus is the national flower of India.
questions.explanation: Lotus is the national flower. True.

questions.text: What is the capital of India?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the capital of India.

questions.text: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is the longest river in India.

questions.text: The Taj Mahal is in the city of ____.
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is in Agra.

questions.text: India got independence on 15th August.
questions.explanation: India became independent on 15 August 1947. True.

questions.text: How many planets are there in our solar system?
questions.explanation: There are 8 planets in our solar system.

questions.text: Which is the closest star to the Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the nearest star to the Earth.

questions.text: Water boils and turns into ____.
questions.explanation: On boiling, water turns into steam (vapour).

questions.text: The Earth moves around the Sun.
questions.explanation: The Earth revolves around the Sun. True.

questions.text: Which is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land animal.

questions.text: Which animal is the tallest?
questions.explanation: The giraffe is the tallest animal.

questions.text: A ____ can live in water and on land.
questions.explanation: A frog is an amphibian — it lives both in water and on land.

questions.text: A camel is called the 'ship of the desert'.
questions.explanation: The camel is called the ship of the desert. True.

questions.text: Which is the first month of the year?
questions.explanation: January is the first month of the year.

questions.text: How many days are there in the month of February (normal year)?
questions.explanation: February has 28 days in a normal year.

questions.text: The last month of the year is ____.
questions.explanation: December is the last month.

questions.text: There are 7 days in a week.
questions.explanation: A week has 7 days. True.

questions.text: Which fruit is known as the 'king of fruits'?
questions.explanation: Mango is called the king of fruits.

questions.text: ____ is a vegetable that makes us cry when we cut it.
questions.explanation: Onion makes our eyes water when cut.

questions.text: Tomato is red when ripe.
questions.explanation: A ripe tomato is red. True.

questions.text: Who is known as the 'Missile Man of India'?
questions.explanation: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam is called the Missile Man of India.

questions.text: ____ Tendulkar is a famous Indian cricketer.
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar is a legendary Indian cricketer.

questions.text: Rabindranath Tagore wrote our national anthem.
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore wrote 'Jana Gana Mana'. True.

questions.text: How many players are there in a cricket team?
questions.explanation: A cricket team has 11 players.

questions.text: ____ is India's national sport (often said to be hockey).
questions.explanation: Hockey is widely regarded as India's national game.

questions.text: We should wash our hands before eating.
questions.explanation: Washing hands before eating keeps germs away. True.

questions.text: Whom should we call in an emergency in India (police)?
questions.explanation: We dial 100 for the police in an emergency.

questions.text: Mixing blue and yellow gives which colour?
questions.explanation: Blue + yellow = green.

questions.text: A shape with six sides is called a hexagon.
questions.explanation: A hexagon has six sides. True.
